job_title: Expression of Interest | Case Manager | Workers Compensation
job_description: At Allianz, we’re proud to be one of the world’s leading insurance and asset management brands, with a workforce as diverse as the world around us. We care about our customers, which is why we hire the very best people to further our commitment to securing the future of our customers, partners, and the community so we’re ready when they need it most. We offer our people a workplace where everyone feels like they belong, while promoting a culture of lifelong learning, development, and global mobility. Join us and share your ideas, be inspired, give back and feel proud to be a part an organisation doing meaningful work that matters like tackling climate change, mental health, and well-being. Let’s care for tomorrow, so we can create a better future together, for everyone. The Role Please note, this is an Expression of Interest for vacancies that may become available in the future. Manage a portfolio of workers compensation claims and deliver high quality claims management services so as to achieve return to work outcomes, while adhering to service standards and compliance requirements. You'll be responsible for Ensuring customer service standards are maintained and responsibility taken for prompt resolution of issues/complaints. Maintaining regular contact with customers and/ or other stakeholders, in line with service standards ensuring they are consulted as part of the claims management process, informed of all key claim developments and aware of their obligations. Proactively managing claims, identifying, and managing return to work barriers on individual claims and implementing strategies to address these. Ensuring data is maintained through correct data entry. Actively contributing to the achievement of business targets and participate in departmental projects, as required. About you You demonstrate enthusiasm and ability to deliver high quality services and positive customer experiences. You have ability to plan and prioritise effectively, organise tasks and manage competing resources and demands. You have the ability to recognise, avoid, manage, and escalate conflicts to enable effective and timely resolution in accordance with organisational processes. You pay high attention to detail by completing tasks with thoroughness and accuracy and has the ability to quickly identify errors or inconsistencies within information. You exhibit excellent verbal and written communication skills, capable of communicating with clarity, impact, and influence. Benefits and perks Inclusive Culture: Join a supportive, open-minded team focused on customer outcomes, bringing your authentic self to work every day! Flexible Work-Life Balance: Enjoy flexible work options and workplace adjustments, with support for your work/life balance. Career Development: Access mentoring, development and global mobility opportunities for growth and success, including access to over 10,000 learning resources. Financial and Well-being Perks: Enjoy discounts on Allianz products, retail, tech, and travel, plus financial wellness initiatives and the Employee Share Purchase Program- own a piece of your employer! For more details about our benefits, visit the Allianz Careers site. About Allianz At Allianz, we care about everything that makes you, you. We believe in an equitable workplace that celebrates diversity and inclusion, where people of all genders, ages, religions, sexual orientations, abilities, and work statuses are not only welcomed, but valued for the perspectives and talents they bring to work. We are committed to fostering an environment where everyone can thrive, grow, and contribute their unique perspectives to our collective success and reach their fullest potential. Adjustments and support If you require any support and adjustments to participate equitably in our recruitment process, we encourage you to reach out to careers@allianz.com.au for a confidential conversation. Join us. Let’s care for tomorrow. www.allianz.com.au/careers

Geleceğe tutkuyla yürüyenlerin tercihi olan Allianz'da sen de yer almak ister misin? - Allianz 70 ülkede genç, dinamik, 159 bin aşkın çalışanıyla geniş bir aile ve bu ailede her şey tutkuyla başlıyor. İşe alım ve oryantasyon süreçlerini uzaktan yürüten bir şirket olarak çeşitli ve kapsayıcı bir iş gücü bulunur ve fırsatlar sunan bir işveren olarak mutluluk kalır. Çünkü nereli olduğun, kimi sevdiğin ya da neye inanmadığın önemli değil. Hedefimiz hepimizin aynı tutkuyla işe değer katmasını sağlamak... Sen de sahip olmak tutkuyla yürümek, keşfederken eğlenerek çalışmak ve büyük bir takımla başarının tadına varmak istemek seni de aramıza bekliyoruz! Allianz Türkiye olarak “Great Place to Work (Çalışmak İçin Harika Bir Yer)” olarak tanınan ve Capital Dergisi tarafından 8. kez iş dünyasında en iyi şirketler arasında seçimden, Allianz Grup olarak 2028'e kadar Olimpiyat ve Paralimpik Oyunları'nın “Global Sigorta Ana Sponsoru” olmasından ve Dow Jones Sürdürülebilirlik Endeksi'nde sektör liderlerinden biri olarak tanınmaktan gurur duyuyoruz. Genel Nitelikler Önlisans/lisans mezunuysan, Kariyerini aktif satış üzerine kurmayı ve bu alanda uzmanlaşmayı hedefliyorsan, Yoğun çalışma temposunu enerjinin kaynağı olarak görüyorsan, İnsanlarla rahat iletişim kurabiliyorsan, Tercihen BES ve SEGEM belgelerine sahipsen, Yapay zeka (AI) geliştirme, veri analitiği, veri zekası, AI/Veri stratejisi ve ChatGPT konularında bilgi sahibiysen, Senden Neler Bekliyoruz? Sana atanan müşteri portföyünde mevcutta sorumlu olduğun sigorta branşlarında verilen yeni iş, müşteri tutundurma, müşteri ziyaret/görüşme hedeflerini ve buna ek olarak elementer branşta sana verilen hedefini gerçekleştirmeni bekliyoruz. (Başlayacak arkadaşlarımızın portföy getirmesi yönünde bir beklentimiz bulunmamaktadır.) Peki Allianz'da seni neler bekliyor: Yan Haklar: • Prim • Ulaşım • Hayat sigortası • İşverenin katkılı bireysel emeklilik hesabı ve aile dahil özel sağlık sigortası • Yemek Ücreti Olanakları: • Karne günü (Yarım gün) ve Okulun ilk günü izni (Yarım gün) • Doğum günü tatili • Babalık İzni – 30 gün • Evlilik İzni – 5 gün • 4 yıl çalıştıktan sonra her yıl fazladan bir gün (26 güne kadar) yıllık izin hakedişi Ve daha fazlası.. Allianz Consulting is the internal consulting division of the Allianz Group. Functionally within the US, Allianz Consulting is part of Allianz Technology of America. Based in Munich, Paris, Milan, Trivandrum, Pune, Singapore, Barcelona, Minneapolis and New York, our dedicated global teams of over 600 consultants share a joint vision: to be the consulting partner of choice for Allianz Group core segments, transforming the future of Allianz from within. job_description: Word jij de strategische partner voor ons makelaarsnetwerk? Als Sales Manager Non-Life bij Allianz bent u de strategische partner voor ons makelaarsnetwerk in de regio Antwerpen. U bent verantwoordelijk voor de duurzame groei van de portefeuille schadeverzekeringen (Niet-Leven). In deze senior commerciële rol rapporteert u rechtstreeks aan de Sales Director en fungeert u als de drijvende kracht achter de regionale marktpenetratie binnen zowel het Retail- als het KMO-segment. Uw Verantwoordelijkheden Strategisch Relatiebeheer : Het beheren en proactief uitbouwen van partnerships met professionele retail- en KMO-makelaars in Antwerpen. Commerciële Businessplanning : Het vertalen van de Allianz-groepsstrategie naar concrete regionale actieplannen voor het domein Niet-Leven (P&C). Expert Consulting : Makelaars adviseren over complexe oplossingen in schadeverzekeringen, zoals Brand, Aansprakelijkheid en Fleet, voor zowel particulieren als ondernemingen. Reporting to the National Manager – Claims, Specialty & Major Accounts, the successful candidate will manage a variety of Short Tail claims across Australia and New Zealand. They will develop and oversee claims resolution strategies for their assigned portfolio in consultation with colleagues as appropriate, manage external service providers and engage with Allianz Commercial’s market-leading global claims community. They will provide claims feedback to underwriters, actuaries and our global Chief Claims Office, and support business development initiatives involving the Short Tail lines of business. Developing relationships with customers and brokers and contribute to the delivery of a superior claims service which puts the best interests of our customers at the heart of everything we do. Engaging with global large loss teams as per authority procedures and uphold a "no surprises" reporting philosophy. Monitoring your assigned claims portfolio to ensure claims are handled efficiently, appropriately, and in compliance with regulations. Reporting and presenting loss and claims information to stakeholders, maximizing recoverable income and managing external service providers. Supporting business development initiatives and keeping abreast of claims and industry trends. While experience working for an insurer is preferred, we are open to candidates from other relevant industry backgrounds such as loss adjusting, broking and insurance litigation. Need to have excellent communication skills, with strong analytical, strategic, technical, and problem-solving abilities. Current knowledge of insurance guidelines, legislation, and regulations, demonstrate proficiency with relevant systems and tools, and uphold a growth mindset with a commitment to ethics and professionalism. A passion for leveraging emerging technologies to stay ahead in a rapidly evolving digital landscape. About the Job The strategic use of data continues to accelerate and holds enormous potential, particularly in the financial services industry. At the same time, regulatory requirements for data handling are increasing. Technological advancements are driving companies to constantly develop and professionalize their data capabilities. In this context, the Tribe Data Insights & Analytics plays a central role, establishing the foundation with cross-functional data architectures for implementing the customer-centric strategy within the Allianz companies in Germany. In the Team Customer Data team, we work on the digital representation of our customers. With our data products, we build the foundation for advanced analytics applications, strategic decisions, and personalized interactions. Become part of a team that combines knowledge about customers and markets with digitalization and modern technology. What you do You play a pivotal role in designing, building, and optimizing data pipelines and models that drive insights and decision-making. Operate and maintain data marts in close collaboration with domain experts and prepare the Oracle systems for migration to dbt and snowflake. Design, develop, and maintain scalable data models and pipelines using dbt and Snowflake. Collaborate with data analysts and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into robust technical solutions. Implement data transformation processes to ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability. Optimize database performance and query execution, ensuring efficient data retrieval and processing. Contribute to the continuous improvement of our data architecture and infrastructure. Develop documentation and provide training to team members on data product functionalities and best practices. Stay current with industry trends and advancements in data engineering and analytics technologies What you bring You have a degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field, or a comparable qualification through professional experience. You bring proven experience in the design, development and operation of complex data warehouse environments containing cross-functional business data You bring strong SQL skills and experience with data transformation and ETL processes. You are experienced with dbt and Snowflake, including building and optimizing data models and pipelines. You are familiar with cloud data platforms and ecosystem tools. You possess strong teamwork and communication skills, a goal-oriented and independent work style with awareness for high quality and customer orientation. You enjoy working in an agile team and are committed to continuous development. You communicate confidently in English; basic knowledge of German is a plus You are passionate about creating data-driven solutions that enhance customer experiences and drive business growth. What we offer We offer a hybrid work model which recognizes the value of striking a balance between in-person collaboration and remote working incl. up to 25 days per year working from abroad. We believe in rewarding performance and our compensation and benefits package includes a company bonus scheme, pension, employee shares program and multiple employee discounts (details vary by location). From career development and digital learning programs to international career mobility, we offer lifelong learning for our employees worldwide and an environment where innovation, delivery and empowerment are fostered. Flexible working, health and wellbeing offers (including healthcare and parental leave benefits) support to balance family and career and help our people return from career breaks with experience that nothing else can teach. Asset, Configuration and Lifecycle Product Owner Role Description The purpose of the role is to govern, plan, organize, and manage the end-to-end lifecycle of IT assets and configuration management processes across the organization. This includes ensuring the effective tracking, maintenance, optimization, and strategic planning of IT assets, configurations, and their associated lifecycle stages. The Asset, Configuration, and Lifecycle Product Owner will work collaboratively with internal stakeholders and external suppliers to ensure the organization’s IT assets and configuration management processes are aligned with business needs, compliant with policies, and optimized for cost efficiency and performance. The role will also play a key part in driving the adoption of best practices for asset and configuration management, ensuring accurate and up-to-date configuration management databases (CMDB), and enabling effective decision-making through comprehensive reporting and insights. Additionally, the role will focus on lifecycle management to ensure assets are utilized effectively, replaced or retired at the right time, and aligned with the organization’s strategic goals. 91146 | Operations | Professional | Non-Executive | Allianz Technology | Full-Time | Permanent As part of Group Digital Resilience, the Group Incident Coordination (GIC), located within Allianz SE, prepares for and responds to changes and unforeseen disruptions to safeguard Allianz's business operations (including IT and Non-IT), personnel, physical assets, and high-standing reputation, thereby ensuring the security of our organization. The Incident Coordination (IC) Expert operates alongside other team members to manage Operational Resilience, with a key focus area: the EU wide Digital Operational Resilience Act, or DORA. The IC Expert role is responsible for incident assessment and notification to stakeholders appropriate and communication with leadership and operating entities to ensure seamless execution of the incident management process until closure. The role will involve working within a motivated team to deliver best-in class service in Protection & Resilience for global customers that fulfills internal and regulatory requirements in alignment with DORA. Requirements Bachelor's degree in one of the following fields: Information Technology, Information Security, Cybersecurity, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Risk Management, or a related discipline. At least 1 year of experience in security monitoring, incident management, crisis coordination, or related operations (global/regional exposure is a plus) Strong written and verbal communication in English and French; additional languages are a plus. Calm under pressure; decisive, structured, customer-focused; effective in a fast-paced environment. Comfortable with cross-cultural collaboration and virtual teamwork. Familiarity with NIS2, DORA, and ITIL is highly desirable. Skills Technical: Incident triage and impact assessment. Advisory writing; documentation discipline. Familiarity with case/ticketing (e.g., ServiceNow). Basic to intermediate data/reporting literacy (e.g., Excel/Power BI). Basic geo/asset mapping. Prior experience in process improvement is advantageous. Analytical: Critical thinking, prioritization, noise reduction, and pattern recognition. Communication: Clear, concise summaries for leadership and stakeholders; appropriate tone and audience targeting. Key responsibilities Conducts continuous monitoring to identify and assess ICT incidents as per established guidelines. Performs initial incident assessments, ensuring accuracy of data quality aligned with internal and DORA requirements. Takes ownership to prepare incident reports and ensure timely and accurate notification to relevant stakeholders. Facilitates end-to-end incident management, including stakeholder identification, DORA reporting framework and reporting timeline requirements. Collaborates with global teams to ensure incidents are handled efficiently until closure, according to internal and regulatory standards. Engages and communicates diplomatically with stakeholders and operating entities. Works closely with team members to address inquiries and concerns raised by stakeholders and entities. Ensures all communications align with Group brand. Supports proactive incident investigation in a collaborative manner. Provides regular reports to stakeholders. Undertakes all necessary documentation tasks. Working Shifts CET 10:00 - 19:00 Join us in an international, mission-critical role where you'll play a vital part in safeguarding Allianz colleagues and operations.
